methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $140M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $5.0B.

data.narrative:
Heron Power is a Series B-stage climate and energy company founded by Drew Baglino, having raised $140M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 9 peers (1 public, 8 private) operating in the same sector and stage, including Commonwealth Fusion Systems, Pano AI, Sage Geosystems, Reverion, and CarbonCapture Inc.

Two valuation methods were applied. The stage-progression approach, using total capital raised as a proxy baseline, applies a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yielding an implied value of $266M. The peer-median method establishes a reference point from six same-sector, same-stage comparables with a median valuation of $355M; this cohort spans a wide range of $120M to $5.0B, reflecting heterogeneity in the climate-tech landscape. 

Triangulating these two approaches produces a valuation range of $226M (low) to $408M (high), with a median of $310M. This range reflects meaningful uncertainty inherent in early-stage climate ventures, where comparable company multiples and growth trajectories vary substantially. The outsized position of Commonwealth Fusion Systems ($5.0B) in the peer set introduces upside scenario consideration but is not the median driver. Key sensitivities include Heron's specific technology maturity, capital efficiency relative to peers, and near-term revenue visibility—factors not quantified in this analysis.
